---
title: "BAA1030 - Data Analytics and Story Telling"
title-block-banner: true
image: BAA1030.jpg
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
---

::: {#hero-heading}

Data and visual analytics is an emerging field concerned with analysing, modelling, and visualising complex high dimensional data. This course will introduce students to the field by covering **state of the art modelling, analysis, and visualisation techniques**. It will emphasise practical challenges involving **complex real world data** and include several case studies and hands on work with **programming languages** (Python and Markdown) and **visualisation software** (Tableau and PowerBI).

:::

---

### Link to the lecture slides:

- [1: Introduction](https://damien-dupre.github.io/BAA1030/lectures/lecture_1)
- [2: Data Collection, Storage and Access](https://damien-dupre.github.io/BAA1030/lectures/lecture_2)
- [3: Principles of Data Visualisation](https://damien-dupre.github.io/BAA1030/lectures/lecture_3)
- [4: Introduction to Tableau](https://damien-dupre.github.io/BAA1030/lectures/lecture_4)
- [5: Advanced Features in Tableau](https://damien-dupre.github.io/BAA1030/lectures/lecture_5)
- [6: Introduction to Python](https://damien-dupre.github.io/BAA1030/lectures/lecture_6)
- [7: Data Wrangling with Polars](https://damien-dupre.github.io/BAA1030/lectures/lecture_7)
- [8: Visualisation in python with plotnine](https://damien-dupre.github.io/BAA1030/lectures/lecture_8)
- [9: Introduction to Quarto](https://damien-dupre.github.io/BAA1030/lectures/lecture_9)
- [10: Publish your Report with GitHub](https://damien-dupre.github.io/BAA1030/lectures/lecture_10)

---

## Student Feedback Survey — Spring 2025

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(plotly)
library(htmltools)

df <- read_csv("../private/BAA1030_feedback.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Extract Likert question columns (Q00_level to which you agree->...)
likert_cols <- grep("level to which you agree", names(df), value = TRUE)

# Clean column names for display
question_labels <- c(
  "Clear objectives",
  "Topics explained well",
  "Topics coherently linked",
  "Lecturer motivates students",
  "Lecturer well prepared",
  "Links to other modules",
  "Intellectually stimulating",
  "Pace satisfactory",
  "Assignments relevant",
  "Advice & support",
  "Overall satisfaction"
)

names(question_labels) <- likert_cols

# Compute means
means <- df |>
  summarise(across(all_of(likert_cols), \(x) round(mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), 2))) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "question", values_to = "mean") |>
  mutate(label = question_labels[question])

n_responses <- nrow(df)
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
This section presents anonymous student feedback collected through DCU's official module evaluation survey (Spring 2025, *n* = `r n_responses`). Quantitative scores use a 5-point Likert scale (1 = Strongly Disagree to 5 = Strongly Agree).
:::

### Overall Satisfaction

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

overall_mean <- means |> filter(label == "Overall satisfaction") |> pull(mean)

plot_ly(
  type = "indicator",
  mode = "gauge+number",
  value = overall_mean,
  height = 280,
  number = list(suffix = " / 5", font = list(size = 28, color = "#003865")),
  gauge = list(
    axis = list(range = list(1, 5), tickwidth = 1, dtick = 1),
    bar = list(color = "#003865", thickness = 0.3),
    bgcolor = "white",
    steps = list(
      list(range = c(1, 2), color = "#c0392b"),
      list(range = c(2, 3), color = "#e07b39"),
      list(range = c(3, 4), color = "#2e7d50"),
      list(range = c(4, 5), color = "#003865")
    ),
    threshold = list(
      line = list(color = "black", width = 3),
      thickness = 0.8,
      value = overall_mean
    )
  )
) |>
  layout(margin = list(t = 40, b = 20, l = 40, r = 40)) |>
  config(displayModeBar = FALSE)
```

### Radar Profile

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# Close the radar polygon by repeating first value
radar_values <- c(means$mean, means$mean[1])
radar_labels <- c(means$label, means$label[1])

plot_ly(
  type = "scatterpolar",
  mode = "lines+markers",
  r = radar_values,
  theta = radar_labels,
  fill = "toself",
  fillcolor = "rgba(0, 56, 101, 0.15)",
  line = list(color = "#003865", width = 2),
  marker = list(color = "#003865", size = 6),
  name = paste0("BAA1030 (n=", n_responses, ")"),
  height = 500,
  hovertemplate = "%{theta}: %{r:.2f}<extra></extra>"
) |>
  layout(
    polar = list(
      radialaxis = list(range = c(1, 5), dtick = 1, gridcolor = "#dde3ed"),
      angularaxis = list(gridcolor = "#dde3ed")
    ),
    showlegend = TRUE,
    legend = list(x = 0.5, xanchor = "center", y = -0.1),
    margin = list(t = 40, b = 60, l = 80, r = 80)
  ) |>
  config(displayModeBar = FALSE)
```

### Response Distribution (Selected Items)

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
#| fig-height: 4

# Pick 4 key items for distribution chart
key_items <- c(
  "Lecturer well prepared",
  "Clear objectives",
  "Pace satisfactory"
)

key_cols <- names(question_labels[question_labels %in% key_items])

dist_data <- df |>
  select(all_of(key_cols)) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "question", values_to = "score") |>
  mutate(
    label = question_labels[question],
    score = factor(score, levels = 1:5)
  ) |>
  count(label, score, .drop = FALSE) |>
  mutate(score_label = case_when(
    score == "1" ~ "1 - Strongly Disagree",
    score == "2" ~ "2 - Disagree",
    score == "3" ~ "3 - Mixed",
    score == "4" ~ "4 - Agree",
    score == "5" ~ "5 - Strongly Agree"
  ))

score_colors <- c("#c0392b", "#e07b39", "#b8a600", "#2e7d50", "#003865")
score_names <- c("1 - Strongly Disagree", "2 - Disagree", "3 - Mixed", "4 - Agree", "5 - Strongly Agree")

p <- plotly::plot_ly()
for (i in seq_along(score_names)) {
  d <- dist_data |> filter(score_label == score_names[i])
  p <- p |> plotly::add_trace(
    data = d, x = ~label, y = ~n,
    type = "bar", name = score_names[i],
    marker = list(color = score_colors[i]),
    hovertemplate = paste0(score_names[i], ": %{y}<extra></extra>")
  )
}
p |>
  plotly::layout(
    barmode = "stack",
    xaxis = list(title = ""),
    yaxis = list(title = "Number of responses", dtick = 1),
    legend = list(orientation = "h", y = -0.2),
    margin = list(b = 100)
  ) |>
  plotly::config(displayModeBar = FALSE)
```

### Student Comments

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

# Extract text columns
strengths_col <- grep("strengths", names(df), value = TRUE)
difficulties_col <- grep("difficulties", names(df), value = TRUE)
improvements_col <- grep("how to improve", names(df), value = TRUE)

strengths <- df[[strengths_col]] |> na.omit() |> trimws()
strengths <- strengths[nchar(strengths) > 0]

difficulties <- df[[difficulties_col]] |> na.omit() |> trimws()
difficulties <- difficulties[nchar(difficulties) > 0]

improvements <- df[[improvements_col]] |> na.omit() |> trimws()
improvements <- improvements[nchar(improvements) > 0]
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

#### {{< fa user-check >}} Strengths

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

for (i in seq_along(strengths)) {
  cat(sprintf(
    '::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}\n*"%s"*\n\n--- Anonymous %d, Spring 2025\n:::\n\n',
    strengths[i], i
  ))
}
```

#### {{< fa circle-exclamation >}} Difficulties

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

for (i in seq_along(difficulties)) {
  cat(sprintf(
    '::: {.callout-warning appearance="minimal"}\n*"%s"*\n\n--- Anonymous %d, Spring 2025\n:::\n\n',
    difficulties[i], i
  ))
}
```

#### {{< fa lightbulb >}} Suggestions

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

for (i in seq_along(improvements)) {
  cat(sprintf(
    '::: {.callout-important appearance="minimal"}\n*"%s"*\n\n--- Anonymous %d, Spring 2025\n:::\n\n',
    improvements[i], i
  ))
}
```

:::
